page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or a GPU, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1897.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 30.32 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 14.47 GB/sec, placing it in the top tier of tested hardware. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 10093.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 13646.63. Memory performance is also strong, reflected in a PassMark memory score of 2866.41 and a cached read-only memory bandwidth of 925.02 GB/sec. However, encryption performance is weak, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 1510.85.

Given its compute-focused architecture and 25 Gbps network baseline, the c8gn.2xlarge is suited for network-intensive and compute-bound workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and text processing. It delivers a balanced profile for ARM64-native applications, offering high memory bandwidth and single-core efficiency. However, it is less suitable for large-scale LLM inference or encryption-heavy tasks due to lower performance in those specific benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1897.0 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression of 14.47 GB/sec. Conversely, encryption performance is weak, scoring 1510.85 in the PassMark encryption test. This instance is suited for network-optimized and compute-bound workloads, such as static web serving, software compilation, and text processing, where ARM64 architecture and high memory bandwidth can be leveraged.
